perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) Live-action video. Two young boys and adults pose with Crayon Shin-chan cutouts in a modern lobby. The boys hold yellow boxes and large cookies. (2) Close-ups of event posters on glass windows and admission tickets are shown. (3) The scene shifts to a bright indoor workshop area with yellow tables; a woman helps a boy sanitize his hands and prepare for a DIY activity. (4) Detailed shots of the cookie-making process: kneading dough, using a rolling pin, and carefully placing black dough pieces to create Shin-chan's facial features. (5) A montage of the children playing in a brightly colored indoor facility with yellow pipes and an electronic drawing station. (6) A boy receives the finished, baked cookie from a counter. (7) The video ends with the boy eating the cookie at a restaurant with his family.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Fast-paced, upbeat pop music with a playful rhythm starts immediately. An adult male voice provides energetic narration in Mandarin. (2) Cartoon-style sound effects like dings, pops, and sparkles are layered over the music. (3) An adult female voice and a young boy's voice are heard interacting during the DIY segment. (4) Ambient noise from the museum (muffled crowd chatter) and the mechanical sound of a scanner/printer are audible. (5) The music continues through to the final scene in the restaurant.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) '大阪限定 DIY 蠟筆小新餅乾' (Decorative/Hook): Bold yellow and red text introducing the limited Osaka DIY activity. (2) '10:30, 11:30, 13:30...' (Functional): Schedule of event times. (3) '門票優惠 1800 日幣' (Functional): Highlighting a discounted ticket price. (4) '製作說明' (Functional): Title for the instructional sequence. (5) '餅乾製作中' (Functional): Captions indicating the process. (6) '真心說餅乾真的很香很好吃' (Decorative): Emotional review text expressing how delicious the cookie is. (7) '憑單領取餅乾' (Functional): Instructional text on how to collect the finished product.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro -> Practical Info -> DIY Process -> Entertainment/Wait -> Final Product/Tasting

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 2 main children (boys), 1 main female creator/mother, 1 staff member at counter, and a few blurred passers-by in the background.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Two young boys in casual winter wear (fleece jackets/vests) and glasses; one adult female in a striped shirt.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Modern Osaka Gas Showroom/Hug Museum interior with large glass windows, workshop tables with yellow branding, and a colorful indoor children's playground. The final scene is in a restaurant with steam rising from a hot pot.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Bright studio lighting and natural daylight through windows.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Information -> Step-by-Step Experience -> Result/Review
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a travel vlog and tutorial guide to promote a limited-time Crayon Shin-chan DIY cookie experience at the Osaka Gas Hug Museum. It aims to showcase the family-friendliness of the venue, the ease of the activity for children, and the overall fun atmosphere of the museum's facilities to encourage viewers to visit.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Detailed display of location, pricing, and specific brand-themed activities (Crayon Shin-chan).
endorsementObject: 大阪ガスハグミュージアム (Osaka Gas Hug Museum)

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Mandarin voiceover with heavy variety-show style text overlays and beat-synced editing.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video effectively targets parents looking for child-friendly activities in Osaka. The high density of text and fast pace are typical of modern short-form travel content.
